perturbation.original_text: 1. For the purposes hereof, Proprietary Information shall be any documents, materials or information that (i) is in tangible form and clearly marked as being proprietary, confidential or sensitive and (ii) is received by one party to this Agreement ("Recipient") from the other party to this Agreement ("Transmitter").
perturbation.changed_text: 1. For the purposes hereof, Proprietary Information shall be any records, materials or information that (i) is in tangible form and clearly marked as being proprietary, confidential or sensitive and (ii) is received by one party to this Agreement ("Recipient") from the other party to this Agreement ("Transmitter").
perturbation.explanation: The word 'documents' is changed to 'records'. This could cause confusion if 'documents' and 'records' are not considered synonymous. Later uses of 'Proprietary Information' might be interpreted differently depending on whether something is considered a document or a record, even if it contains the same information. For instance, if UNISYS and Celerity have different understandings on what consitutes a record versus a document, this can cause a contradiction. The specific contradiction arises in the following sections where the term 'Proprietary Information' is used, but its definition becomes uncertain due to the interchangeable use of 'records' versus documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights and obligations of the Parties to this Agreement shall be subject to and governed by the terms of this Agreement and other documents or specifications attached hereto or Referenced herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'documents' to 'records' in the definition of 'Proprietary Information' introduces a potential ambiguity. The contradicted text refers to 'documents' in a general sense, and if 'records' and 'documents' are not considered synonymous within the context of the agreement between UNISYS and Celerity, the modified definition of 'Proprietary Information' directly contradicts the reference to 'documents' in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about which items are governed by the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The Recipient agrees to safeguard, protect and handle all Proprietary Information made available under this Agreement with the same degree of care and procedures as Recipient applies to its own confidential or proprietary information. Without the written consent of the Transmitter, the Recipient of Proprietary Information agrees (i) not to disclose, disseminate, reproduce or otherwise distribute such information and materials to any third party, except a governmental judicial authority's issued order and (ii) not to use such information for any other purposes beyond that expressly set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Recipient agrees to safeguard, protect and handle all Proprietary Data made available under this Agreement with the same degree of care and procedures as Recipient applies to its own confidential or proprietary information. Without the written consent of the Transmitter, the Recipient of Proprietary Information agrees (i) not to disclose, disseminate, reproduce or otherwise distribute such information and materials to any third party, except a governmental judicial authority's issued order and (ii) not to use such information for any other purposes beyond that expressly set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Change 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence but retain 'Proprietary Information' in the second sentence of this paragraph. Because of this in-text change of terminology, there is a contradiction to what constitutes the main point of data to be protected, is it Data or Information?. This leads to uncertainty about which standard applies to the handled data. Further uses of 'Proprietary Information' are now questionable because of this perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Recipient agrees to safeguard, protect and handle all Proprietary Data made available under this Agreement with the same degree of care and procedures as Recipient applies to its own confidential or proprietary information. Without the written consent of the Transmitter, the Recipient of Proprietary Information agrees (i) not to disclose, disseminate, reproduce or otherwise distribute such information and materials to any third party, except a governmental judicial authority's issued order and (ii) not to use such information for any other purposes beyond that expressly set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence, while retaining 'Proprietary Information' in the second sentence within the same paragraph, creates a direct conflict. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, leads to uncertainty about which type of data is being referred to and protected. Since the changed text is the same as the contradicted text, the inconsistency exists within the text itself, fulfilling the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Transmitter. It is agreed that the Recipient does not obtain any title or license rights to the Proprietary Information furnished under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Transmitter. It is agreed that the Recipient does not obtain any title or license rights to the Proprietary Information furnished under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Change 'Proprietary Information' to 'Confidential Information'. While both are similar, by changing Proprietary to Confidential, now the user is left wondering what exactly is the difference, given the fact that both were named in the earlier Definitions section as Proprietary or Confidential, causing contradiction, by implying there is a difference between the two and making the user think about what information is Confidential or Proprietary. This inconsistency leads to potential disputes over ownership and usage rights. The contradiction affects any section referencing these rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Transmitter. It is agreed that the Recipient does not obtain any title or license rights to the Proprietary Information furnished under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Confidential Information' in the changed text, while the contradicted text retains both terms, introduces ambiguity and potential conflict. The explanation states that this change implies a difference between the two terms where clarity is needed, thus creating a contradiction by causing confusion about what information is covered under each term. This is because both terms were initially defined in the Definitions section. Therefore the changed text creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text as per Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4. All Proprietary Information exchanged under this Agreement shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with written instructions upon the earlier of a written request from the Transmitter or termination of this Agreement. Either party may terminate this Agreement, at any time, by providing written notice to the receiving party. Termination of this Agreement shall not be construed as relieving the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect and dispose of the Proprietary Information as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All Sensitive Information exchanged under this Agreement shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with written instructions upon the earlier of a written request from the Transmitter or termination of this Agreement. Either party may terminate this Agreement, at any time, by providing written notice to the receiving party. Termination of this Agreement shall not be construed as relieving the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect and dispose of the Proprietary Information as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Proprietary Information' is replaced with 'Sensitive Information' in the first sentence, creating inconsistency within the same paragraph. Now there is a difference between the scope of 'Sensitive Information' versus the scope of 'Proprietary Information' making the reader wonder what exactly should be returned or destroyed and the what exactly should be safeguarded. The obligation to safeguard, protect, and dispose of 'Proprietary Information' is now uncertain due to the different terminology used for what must be returned. Further sections that invoke obligations related to safeguarding Proprietary Information are now questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. All Sensitive Information exchanged under this Agreement shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with written instructions upon the earlier of a written request from the Transmitter or termination of this Agreement. Either party may terminate this Agreement, at any time, by providing written notice to the receiving party. Termination of this Agreement shall not be construed as relieving the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect and dispose of the Proprietary Information as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Proprietary Information' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in the first sentence but retains 'Proprietary Information' in the third sentence of the same paragraph. This creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces two different terms, 'Sensitive Information' and 'Proprietary Information', within the same paragraph, raising questions about their respective scopes and creating uncertainty regarding which information is subject to the safeguard obligations, thus adhering to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
